content:
🔥 SigDigger | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Analyzing and extracting information from unknown radio signals using SDR hardware.
✨ Key Features: • Digital signal analysis • Support for various SDR devices via SoapySDR • Adjustable demodulation of FSK, PSK, and ASK signals • Analog video decoding • Analysis of bursty signals
📖 Summary: SigDigger is a free, Qt-based digital signal analyzer for GNU/Linux and macOS that extracts information from unknown radio signals. It supports various SDR devices via SoapySDR and offers adjustable demodulation for FSK, PSK, and ASK signals, along with analog video decoding and voice channel listening.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 Look4Sat | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: Tracking and predicting satellite passes on an Android device.
✨ Key Features: • Predicting satellite positions and passes for up to 10 days • Showing the list of currently active and upcoming satellite passes • Showing the active pass progress, polar trajectory and transceivers info • Showing the satellite positional data, footprint and ground track on the map
📖 Summary: Look4Sat is an Android application that tracks satellites and predicts their passes. Inspired by Gpredict, it uses data from Celestrak and SatNOGS to provide real-time positional data, pass predictions, and ground track visualizations for over 9000 satellites.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 AndroidForensics | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automated extraction of digital artifacts from Android devices via ADB for forensic analysis, security audits, and investigations.
✨ Key Features: • Extract device system information (model, OS version, serial number) • List and export installed applications • Collect system logs (logcat) • Retrieve battery and power data • Dump network configuration and connection details
📖 Summary: AndroidForensics is a toolkit and guide that leverages ADB commands to extract various data artifacts from Android devices for security audits and investigations. It focuses on gathering system information, installed applications, logs, network data, and user activity without requiring root access on non-rooted devices.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 taste-skill | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To elevate the quality and aesthetic appeal of AI-generated frontend user interfaces by providing specialized skills that go beyond generic design.
✨ Key Features: • Portable Agent Skills for AI interfaces • Improves layout, typography, motion, and spacing • Generates image assets for reference boards (web, mobile, brand kits)
📖 Summary: Taste Skill is a framework for AI agents that enhances the quality of AI-generated frontend interfaces. It provides portable 'Agent Skills' to improve layout, typography, motion, and spacing, moving beyond generic UI designs. It also includes image-generation skills for creating reference boards and design assets.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
